topic: Unequal-fold cross-fitting phase threshold for DML causal estimators

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the unequal- or growing-fold cross-fitting phase threshold for orthogonal DML/AIPW estimators when the evaluation-fold mass spectrum c_{k,n}=|I_{k,n}|/n is nonuniform or triangular.

source_refs.claim: The original DML algorithms define K fixed folds of equal size n/K and obtain asymptotic linearity under that split geometry.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, and Robins

source_refs.claim: Velez studies DML under a growing-K framework and shows that fixed-K equivalence of DML1 and DML2 no longer gives guidance once K changes with n.
source_refs.author: Velez

source_refs.claim: DoubleML restates DML1/DML2 with K-fold partitions whose folds have size n/K and exposes score values for inference.
source_refs.author: Bach, Chernozhukov, Kurz, Spindler, and Klaassen

source_refs.excerpt: Add Velez2024DML to Related work and the Conjecture 1 why-new block
Velez2024DML separates DML1 and DML2 under growing

open_problems.why_unsolved: Published DML and DoubleML references route the first-order law through fixed, equal-size folds, while Velez and the prior reviewer record show that growing-K already changes DML1/DML2 comparisons. The missing object is not another product-rate condition, but a fold-mass-spectrum theorem in which unequal evaluation weights enter the leading empirical process and determine whether the standard DML report remains valid.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to apply the fixed-K DML theorem verbatim fails because Chernozhukov et al. and DoubleML normalize the algorithms with |I_k|=n/K, while the reviewer evidence points to Velez-style growing-K behavior that does not collapse to the old equal-fold asymptotics.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A phase theorem giving necessary or sufficient fold-geometry conditions for root-n asymptotic linearity, variance inflation, or strict DML report separation under a limiting fold-mass spectrum.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the boundary at which a vanishing minimum evaluation-fold mass turns orthogonal-score DML from full root-n asymptotic linearity into slower small-fold or leave-one-out/no-splitting regimes.

source_refs.claim: The ddml package treats any finite K as theoretically admissible, implements approximately equal folds, exposes custom fold variables, and recommends more work on choosing K.
source_refs.author: Ahrens, Hansen, Schaffer, and Wiemann

source_refs.claim: Growing K can separate DML1 and DML2 and makes K=n asymptotically optimal for scalar DML2 in a second-order bias/MSE criterion.
source_refs.author: Velez

source_refs.claim: Debiased ML can dispense with sample splitting only when the nuisance learners satisfy leave-one-out stability properties.
source_refs.author: Chen, Syrgkanis, and Austern

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature has fixed finite-K cross-fitting, growing-K comparisons, and no-splitting theory under learner stability, but it does not characterize the intermediate triangular-array path where one or more evaluation folds shrink while the training fold approaches the full sample. That path is exactly where a standard fold average, a small-fold empirical process, and a leave-one-out stability argument may give different first-order approximations.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to let K grow inside the finite-K argument fails because Ahrens et al. only justify finite K and Chen-Syrgkanis-Austern replace cross-fitting with a separate leave-one-out stability condition.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A phase theorem specifying rates, limiting distributions, and variance behavior as functions of min_k c_{k,n} and K_n, including a boundary case for leave-one-out or no-splitting limits.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a valid standard-error formula for deterministic, custom, or repeated unbalanced DML cross-fitting folds when the usual score-variance report ignores fold-mass and split-randomness components.

source_refs.claim: The DML paper proposes repeated sample splitting because the random partition can matter in finite samples, even though K is asymptotically immaterial under its equal-fold conditions.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, and Robins

source_refs.claim: ddml reports conventional standard errors after cross-fitting, supports custom fold variables, and recommends repeated cross-fitting to assess split randomness.
source_refs.author: Ahrens, Hansen, Schaffer, and Wiemann

source_refs.claim: Changing the DML sampling geometry to multiway clusters required a new cross-fitting algorithm and a new cluster-robust standard-error formula.
source_refs.author: Chiang, Kato, Ma, and Sasaki

open_problems.why_unsolved: Standard DML variance formulas are tied to equal-fold i.i.d. score averaging, while software interfaces expose custom folds and repeated partitions. Existing geometry changes such as multiway-cluster DML show that the standard-error formula can change once the resampling design changes, but there is no fold-mass-indexed variance estimator for unbalanced deterministic or random cross-fitting folds.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to report the usual empirical score variance over n fails because Ahrens et al. attach that report to approximately equal folds and Chernozhukov et al. separately add repeated-split adjustments for split randomness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A plug-in or sandwich variance-estimation theorem that remains valid under arbitrary fold-mass arrays and repeated cross-fitting aggregation.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the joint allocation frontier linking unequal fold masses, nuisance tuning rates, and orthogonal-score remainder rates for doubly robust DML functionals.

source_refs.claim: McGrath and Mukherjee show that nuisance tuning choices and sample-splitting strategies jointly determine optimal rates for doubly robust functionals.
source_refs.author: McGrath and Mukherjee

source_refs.claim: Newey and Robins use cross-fitting to obtain fast remainder rates for semiparametric estimators and show different cross-fit constructions can have different remainder behavior.
source_refs.author: Newey and Robins

source_refs.claim: The finite-sample DML theorem translates learner rates into inference conditions for sample-split debiased estimators but does not make unequal fold allocation the theorem object.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh

open_problems.why_unsolved: Recent work shows that sample splitting and nuisance tuning jointly control doubly robust rates, while finite-sample DML theorems provide rate conversion once the split geometry is fixed. The open regime is a fold-allocation frontier in which the train/evaluation mass spectrum changes the nuisance-rate remainder and therefore changes the boundary between root-n inference, slower inference, and failure.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to choose folds after verifying nuisance rates fails because McGrath and Mukherjee show the sample-splitting strategy and nuisance tuning are coupled under low regularity.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A rate-conversion theorem giving inequalities over fold masses and nuisance convergence rates that imply root-n DML inference, slower non-root-n inference, or invalid asymptotics.

literature_map:
Chernozhukov et al. (2018, Econometrics Journal) is the central DML anchor: Neyman-orthogonal scores plus cross-fitting remove regularization and overfitting bias under fixed K and equal folds.
Chernozhukov et al. (2017, AER Papers and Proceedings) gives the treatment-effect DML illustration using orthogonal scores and cross-fitting.
The DML paper assumes equal folds of size n/K and treats K as asymptotically immaterial under those conditions.
Bach et al. (2024, JSS) implements DoubleML and restates DML1/DML2 with equal K-fold partitions and score-based inference.
Ahrens et al. (2024, Stata Journal) is operationally important because ddml supports custom fold variables, repeated cross-fitting, and finite-K practice, while still using approximately equal folds in the theory-facing exposition.
Velez (2024, arXiv/working paper; latest version 2026) directly opens the growing-K flank by separating DML1 and DML2 once fixed-K equivalence no longer applies.
Chernozhukov et al. (2018) already treats split randomness as finite-sample relevant and proposes repeated-split adjustments, leaving room for an asymptotic split-geometry variance theorem.
Newey and Robins (2018, arXiv) develops cross-fitting and fast remainder rates for semiparametric estimation, showing that the construction of the split can affect remainder behavior.
McGrath and Mukherjee (2022, arXiv) studies nuisance tuning together with sample-splitting strategies for doubly robust functionals, supporting a fold-allocation/rate frontier gap.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2023, Biometrika) gives finite-sample DML guarantees translating learner rates into inference conditions, but not an unequal-fold phase diagram.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrica) develops automatic debiased ML and robust standard errors, but split geometry remains background rather than the theorem object.
Chiang, Kato, Ma, and Sasaki (2022, JBES) shows that changing DML sampling geometry to multiway clusters can require a new cross-fitting algorithm and variance formula.
Chen, Syrgkanis, and Austern (2022, arXiv) gives a contrasting endpoint: no sample splitting is valid only under leave-one-out stability of the nuisance learners.
The supplied study-pipeline gaps concerned LATE, Manski bounds, and interval regression; they did not cross-link to the DML fold-spectrum topic and were not retained.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the active formalization tree under doc/formalization; the active qid eid_dml_fold_spectrum_phase currently has only state/pipeline files, so no same-qid prior proposal text was available.
Highest-yield adjacent qids were eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1, eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2, and eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1.
Reviewer JSONs for eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1 supplied strengthening paths around Velez2024DML growing-K DML1/DML2 and spectral-threshold separation.
The Velez-related reviewer path and rejected angle were cross-linked to the web literature scan and retained as the sole source=both entry.
The failed eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 proposals supplied studentization-report and LRO-normalization open kernels, but those are adjacent report-equivalence gaps rather than the current unequal-fold phase target.
The downgraded eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1 reviews supplied empirical Gram-diagnostic and Riesz critical-radius DML gaps, but those were not retained after topic-specific G1-G6 filtering.
Bank README evidence marked eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 failed and eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1 downgraded, so these were treated as prior open-proposal substrates rather than accepted results.
